title: Homer Defined

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Episode opening, Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.punchline: The iconic D'oh! that defines Homer's character
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific catchphrase, maximum economy, culturally iconic

jokes.text: S.A.T. scores are declining at a slower rate
jokes.setup: Marge reads what she thinks is 'good news' from the paper
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of celebrating that test scores are declining 'slower'
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of optimistic spin on declining education standards

jokes.text: This paper is a flimsy hodgepodge of pie graphs, factoids and Larry King
jokes.setup: Homer criticizing a newspaper
jokes.punchline: Treating Larry King as equivalent to pie graphs and factoids
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic treating a person as newspaper content

jokes.text: This is the only paper in America not afraid to tell the truth--that everything is just fine
jokes.setup: Homer praising the paper for telling 'the truth'
jokes.punchline: The 'truth' being willful ignorance that everything is fine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - preferring comforting lies over difficult truths

jokes.text: Eww. Come on. Open it! Open it!
jokes.setup: Milhouse reacting with disgust to something in Bart's card
jokes.punchline: The contrast between his disgust and immediate enthusiasm for the gift
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing kid's conflicted reactions

jokes.text: Whenever you want to talk to me... call me on the phone... and tell me to turn on my walkie-talkie
jokes.setup: Bart explaining how the walkie-talkies work
jokes.punchline: Completely defeating the purpose of walkie-talkies by requiring a phone call first
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd logic that completely undermines the gift's purpose

jokes.text: Hey, look at that dog. Isn't that something? Wow! Brown.
jokes.setup: Otto trying to distract from the party conversation
jokes.punchline: His stoned amazement at the most mundane observation about a dog
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto character beat - stoner fascination with the obvious

jokes.text: Don't learn anything... I wouldn't learn
jokes.setup: Otto giving parting advice to students
jokes.punchline: Encouraging ignorance while accidentally revealing his own
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing Otto's self-aware ignorance

jokes.text: Time to pay... for your two days of debauchery... you hung-over drones
jokes.setup: Burns addressing workers on Monday morning
jokes.punchline: His theatrical contempt for workers having weekends
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns villainy - treating normal weekend activities as moral failings

jokes.text: T.G.I.M., sir
jokes.setup: Smithers responding to Burns' Monday morning rant
jokes.punchline: Thank God It's Monday - the opposite of normal human sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Smithers sycophancy - inverting normal expressions to please Burns

jokes.text: Who the devil is Hercules? My Yorkshire terrier. He's tiny. You know, it's a joke.
jokes.setup: Burns asks about Hercules, expecting something impressive
jokes.punchline: It's a tiny dog with an ironic name, and Smithers has to explain the joke
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Smithers' mundane life and need to explain humor to Burns

jokes.text: Well, don't you know how to paint the town red
jokes.setup: Burns hearing about Smithers' boring weekend
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about the most mundane activities imaginable
jokes.explanation: Standard Burns sarcasm, well-delivered but not particularly original

jokes.text: Give the great unwashed... a pair of oversized breasts and a happy ending... and they'll oink for more
jokes.setup: Burns continuing his movie critique
jokes.punchline: Reducing audiences to pigs while revealing his own crude focus
jokes.explanation: Sharp Burns elitism with perfect character-specific vulgarity

jokes.text: What a movie! And that blonde-- does she have assets! Heh-heh-heh!
jokes.setup: Burns' immediate contradiction of his moral outrage
jokes.punchline: Gleefully praising the same movie he just condemned
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the previous setup showing Burns' complete hypocrisy

jokes.text: Eww! Lemon. Ugh! Cherry. Ooh... custard. Mmm... purple!
jokes.setup: Homer trying different donuts
jokes.punchline: Progressing from real flavors to just identifying a color as delicious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating color as flavor, shows his simple food appreciation

jokes.text: Got a bad case of the munchies, man. Time for a heat-lamp dog
jokes.setup: Otto explaining his convenience store visit
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of drug use and terrible food choices
jokes.explanation: Standard Otto stoner humor, well-executed but predictable

jokes.text: Did you know there's a child in your bus? Good thing you warned me. I was heading to Mexico.
jokes.setup: Apu pointing out Bart is still on the school bus
jokes.punchline: Otto was about to drive to Mexico with a student aboard
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation showing Otto's complete incompetence as bus driver

jokes.text: Actually, he was hired under 'Project Bootstrap.' Thank you, President Ford.
jokes.setup: Burns asking if Homer is intelligent
jokes.punchline: Homer was hired through an incompetency program, with sarcastic political blame
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about affirmative action programs while explaining Homer's employment

jokes.text: Whosever problem this is, I'm sure they can handle it
jokes.setup: Nuclear warning sounds are going off
jokes.punchline: Homer assuming someone else will handle the crisis he's responsible for
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer irresponsibility - avoiding ownership of his own crisis

jokes.text: It's my problem! We're doomed!
jokes.setup: Homer's sudden realization he's the safety inspector
jokes.punchline: Immediate panic and assumption of total failure
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to previous setup showing Homer's rapid emotional swings

jokes.text: My old lady ran off and married my brother. It hurt, but it's a month later, and I'm sleeping on their couch
jokes.setup: Otto trying to console Bart about friendship problems
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing his bizarre living situation with ex and brother
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy escalation that's both shocking and perfectly Otto-like

jokes.text: Mr. Devereaux, I can't work under these conditions. Have it your way, baby. You're fired. Oh, Avery. Call me Mr. Devereaux.
jokes.setup: Soap opera scene with workplace harassment
jokes.punchline: Demanding formal address immediately after sexual harassment
jokes.explanation: Good soap opera parody showing absurd workplace dynamics

jokes.text: We prefer to call it an 'unrequested fission surplus'
jokes.setup: Reporter mentions 'meltdown'
jokes.punchline: Burns' euphemistic corporate spin on nuclear disaster
jokes.explanation: Brilliant corporate doublespeak that perfectly captures bureaucratic spin on disasters

jokes.text: It's as fat as a phone book!
jokes.setup: Homer finds the reactor manual during a crisis
jokes.punchline: His amazement at the manual's size rather than urgency of the situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer mispriorities - focusing on irrelevant details during crisis

jokes.text: Who'd have thought a nuclear reactor would be so complicated?
jokes.setup: Homer struggling with the reactor manual
jokes.punchline: Genuine surprise that nuclear technology is complex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer naivety about basic scientific complexity

jokes.text: There may never be another time to say... I love you, sir. Oh, hot dog. Thank you for making my last few moments socially awkward.
jokes.setup: Smithers making a deathbed confession
jokes.punchline: Burns' disgusted reaction to emotional intimacy even facing death
jokes.explanation: Perfect character interaction showing Burns' emotional coldness even in crisis

jokes.text: When they look up 'stupid' in a dictionary... there will be a picture of me
jokes.setup: Homer lamenting his incompetence
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating joke using familiar phrase structure
jokes.explanation: Standard self-deprecating humor, well-delivered but not particularly original

jokes.text: Oh, the next time there's a canned food drive... I'll give the poor something they'd actually like... instead of old lima beans and pumpkin mix
jokes.setup: Reverend making desperate bargain with God
jokes.punchline: His idea of charity improvement is still hilariously inadequate
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about performative charity and religious bargaining

jokes.text: This button controls the emergency override. In a meltdown, push this button, and only this button. Ooh, a side. - Simpson! - What? You see which button? Yeah, yeah, push the button. Got it.
jokes.setup: Critical safety instruction during flashback
jokes.punchline: Homer getting distracted by lunch during life-saving information
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment showing his inability to focus on important information

jokes.text: One potato, two potato-- No, wait. Bubble gum, bubble gum, in a dish. How many pieces do you wish?
jokes.setup: Homer using children's counting rhymes to choose which button to press
jokes.punchline: Switching between different nonsensical selection methods during nuclear crisis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist approach to life-or-death decision making

jokes.text: I guess there's nothing left... but to kiss my sorry ass good-bye. May I, sir?
jokes.setup: Homer using vulgar expression about dying
jokes.punchline: Smithers taking it literally and asking permission
jokes.explanation: Perfect Smithers character moment - literal interpretation of metaphor plus sycophancy

jokes.text: Eeny, meeny, miney, moe. Catch a tiger by the toe. If he hollers, let him go. Eeny, meeny, miney... ...moe.
jokes.setup: Homer using final childhood rhyme to make nuclear decision
jokes.punchline: Complete the rhyme as countdown reaches zero, pure chance saves everyone
jokes.explanation: Masterful climax - childhood rhyme accidentally saves the day, perfect absurdist resolution

jokes.text: Three loins in the fountain
jokes.setup: Wheel of Fortune puzzle solving
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'coins' as 'loins' making it sexual
jokes.explanation: Decent game show parody but somewhat cheap sexual humor

jokes.text: It seems a single wayward crow... flew into our warning system
jokes.setup: Burns explaining the nuclear crisis on TV
jokes.punchline: Blaming a crow for the false alarm rather than admitting real problems
jokes.explanation: Brilliant corporate deflection - blaming wildlife for systemic failures

jokes.text: Now we're even for your party
jokes.setup: Bart getting revenge on Milhouse by putting salt in his eye
jokes.punchline: Treating assault as equivalent payback for not being invited to a party
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Bart's disproportionate sense of justice

jokes.text: A nuclear reactor is a lot like a woman-- you just read the manual and press the right button
jokes.setup: Homer giving wisdom about his heroic moment
jokes.punchline: Terrible analogy comparing women to machines with manuals
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer sexist wisdom that reveals his complete misunderstanding of both topics

jokes.text: A congratulatory phone call from Earvin 'Magic' Johnson?
jokes.setup: Burns announcing a special honor for Homer
jokes.punchline: Random celebrity involvement in nuclear plant business
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist element - celebrity involvement in mundane workplace recognition

jokes.text: Magic, what if people think a guy's a hero... but he was just lucky? Sooner or later people like that are exposed as frauds.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Magic for advice about fake heroes
jokes.punchline: Magic unknowingly describing exactly what will happen to Homer
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony - celebrity unknowingly predicting Homer's downfall

jokes.text: Tastes so bitter, it's like ashes. It's actually more of a honey glaze. Maybe you ate a clove.
jokes.setup: Homer saying his victory ham tastes bitter due to guilt
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical explanation destroying his metaphorical moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character interaction - Marge's practicality vs Homer's dramatic guilt

jokes.text: Looking at you with quiet awe. As long as it's quiet.
jokes.setup: Lisa admiring her heroic father
jokes.punchline: Homer only caring about the quiet part, not the admiration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting - caring more about peace than daughter's admiration

jokes.text: Help them find their eanae-eharatou-nah-duleve. Their eanae-haratou-doola what? We want you to give them a pep talk... that turns them from donut-eating goof-offs... into Homer Simpsons.
jokes.setup: Greek plant owner using complex Greek phrase
jokes.punchline: Homer can't understand it, Burns translates as becoming 'Homer Simpsons' - the ultimate irony
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - Homer being held up as the ideal worker when he's incompetent

jokes.text: Let's go throw rocks at that hornets' nest
jokes.setup: Bart suggesting an activity after being told not to play with Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Immediately suggesting dangerous activity that proves he's a bad influence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - immediately proving his mother's point about being a bad influence

jokes.text: He must have gotten that from your little boy... because they certainly don't say that word on TV
jokes.setup: Mrs. Van Houten explaining why Bart is a bad influence
jokes.punchline: Her naive belief that TV doesn't contain profanity
jokes.explanation: Good parental naivety humor about media influence

jokes.text: In the Christmas pageant, they're always sheep
jokes.setup: Marge explaining why Bart and Milhouse need each other
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about their low status in school hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific detail that perfectly captures childhood social dynamics

jokes.text: I pictured everyone in their underwear-- the judge, the jury, my lawyer-- everybody. Did it work? I'm a free man.
jokes.setup: Barney giving advice about overcoming nervousness during speeches
jokes.punchline: Implying the underwear trick helped him get acquitted in court
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy twist on common advice - suggesting criminal context

jokes.text: You are not workers. You are a pack... of mangy, cud-chewing, ugly goats
jokes.setup: Greek plant owner beginning what should be motivational speech
jokes.punchline: Insulting workers as livestock instead of inspiring them
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing terrible management approach

jokes.text: Eeny, meeny, miney, moe. Catch a tiger by toe. If he hollers, let him go. Eeny, meeny, miney... moe.
jokes.setup: Second nuclear crisis, Homer using same method
jokes.punchline: Identical approach works again, proving it wasn't skill
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that reinforces the absurdity and proves Homer's luck

jokes.text: Do you even know what button you pushed?! Sure-- moe.
jokes.setup: Greek owner demanding technical explanation
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks the button is literally named after his rhyme
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - assuming technical equipment is named after children's rhymes

jokes.text: Is Homer a hero? The answer is no. I'm Kent Brockman and that was 'My Two Cents.'
jokes.setup: News anchor's editorial segment
jokes.punchline: Blunt dismissal of Homer's heroism with generic sign-off
jokes.explanation: Good media satire showing news anchors' arbitrary editorial opinions

jokes.text: It seems the chief had gone to the bank... to cash in his penny jar. ...48, 49, 50. What the-- ? Good work, Chief. Just doing my job.
jokes.setup: Police chief accidentally foiling bank robbery
jokes.punchline: He was there for mundane penny-counting, takes credit anyway
jokes.explanation: Good parallel to Homer's situation - accidental heroism with credit taken

jokes.text: Yes, it seems the chief pulled a Homer Simpson of his own
jokes.setup: Reporter describing Chief Wiggum's accidental success
jokes.punchline: Homer's name becoming slang for accidental success
jokes.explanation: Brilliant concept - character's name becoming generic term, perfect setup for montage

jokes.text: Looks like I pulled a Homer
jokes.setup: Vendor having accidental success
jokes.punchline: Using Homer's name as the new expression
jokes.explanation: Part of running gag montage, effective but repetitive by design

jokes.text: Looks like I pulled a Homer
jokes.setup: Magic Johnson's accidental three-point play
jokes.punchline: Famous athlete using Homer's expression
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Magic Johnson from earlier, shows expression's spread

jokes.text: Looks like I pulled a Homer.
jokes.setup: Magic Johnson accidentally scoring during a slip
jokes.punchline: Even a professional athlete using Homer's name for accidental success
jokes.explanation: Nice callback using Magic Johnson from earlier in the episode. Shows the phrase has truly entered the culture.

jokes.text: Our dad! Now he belongs to the ages
jokes.setup: Kids watching their father become culturally significant
jokes.punchline: Grandiose historical language for silly pop culture phenomenon
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - treating Homer's linguistic contribution with mock historical importance

tmdb_overview: Homer's averting of a nuclear meltdown earns him employee-of-the-month honors--and a call from Magic Johnson; Milhouse's parents prohibit him from playing with Bart.

guest_stars.name: Pamela Hayden
guest_stars.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Shelbyville Plant Computer Voice (voice)

insight: Homer's nuclear meltdown averages 3 jokes per minute—Season 3's densest episode yet.
